description: Fair cross-lab comparison scenario with fixed duration and payload size.

comparison_profile.objective: Scientific cross-lab comparability with fixed payload, duration, and load shape.

workload_model.payload_shape: Fixed-size chat payload with trace_id, message_id, client_send_ts, user_id, room_id, and padded content.
workload_model.concurrency_model: Deterministic virtual users with one message every 1000 ms in the comparison run.

metrics_contract.latency: Client-observed end-to-end latency from client_send_ts to echoed message receipt.
metrics_contract.throughput: Server-side processed messages per second from Prometheus counters.
metrics_contract.error_rate: Dropped messages plus DB errors divided by total processed messages.
metrics_contract.db_latency: Database query latency when a lab exposes a persistence path.

traceability.contract: trace_id and message_id are generated by the client and echoed by the server with server_receive_ts and server_broadcast_ts when available.

consistency_model.notes: One in-memory authority; no distributed replication or persistence semantics.

failure_model.focus: Local process overload and mutex contention.

routing_strategy.objective: Establish the latency floor before any distributed indirection.

observability.baseline: Prometheus metrics plus per-run benchmark artifacts.
observability.logging: Message IDs are visible through the benchmark harness and sample traces.

cost_model.dominant_axis: CPU and memory on one node.
cost_model.notes: No network egress or external service cost yet.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Shared in-memory state is fast but non-durable and non-scalable.
synthesis.learning_goal: Establish the baseline latency floor and the first comparable workload.

synthesis.learning_goal: Establish the baseline latency floor and the first comparable workload.

comparison_standard.description: Fair cross-lab comparison scenario with fixed duration and payload size.

baseline.description: Steady load to capture the monolith operating curve.

saturation.description: Sustained pressure to expose mutex and broadcast bottlenecks.

spike_recovery.description: Abrupt spike and recovery to observe tail latency and memory rebound.
